solutions.solution:
Third Output: sherry quart bar pint
Obviously, the next step will be consisting of Operation Two and Four as they are to be consecutively performed on 3rd and 4th place. Alternatively mentioned, we will do the two words using and interchanging the last two words as per the positions of the input quart pint bar.
If the machine went on, the sequence would be generated as shown:
Input: sherry quart pint bar
Step I: quart sherry pint bar
Step II: quart sherry bar pint
Step III: sherry quart bar pint
Step IV: sherry quart pint bar
Step V: quart sherry pint bar
Step VI: quart sherry bar pint
Note that we perform operation One (interchanging only the first two words) and operation Two (interchanging only the last two words) alternatively.
Ex 2: We will now look at a different type of operation performed by a different machine.
Input: sherry quart pint bar
Now, suppose that the machine does the following operation on this input:
It scans the words given, it then looks for the word that comes first in a dictionary among all the words and puts in the first place. So, the 1st output:
1st output: bar sherry quart pint
Explanation: Since "bar" is alphabetically the first word, therefore it is put in the first place. The remaining words are pushed to the right without changing their order.
If the machine went on with its logic, it is obvious that the second output will be:
2nd Output: bar pint sherry quart
Explanation: Since "pint" is alphabetically the second word (and bar is already in place), "pint" is put in the second place. Remaining words are shifted to the right without any change in their order.
And then the Third Output: bar pint quart sherry
3rd Output: bar pint quart sherry
Explanation: Since "quart" is the alphabetically the third word after bar and pint, it comes to the third place. The remaining word is sherry and automatically occupies its due place that is fourth.
Note: Please note here the 2nd Output is arranged in correct alphabetical order and therefore this is the last step and the machine stopped here!
Ex 3: We will now have a look at yet another type of operation. Suppose we begin with our input:
Input: sherry quart pint bar

PROBLEMS:
Let us suppose that the machine performs a set of random operations as given below.
Step I: the last two letters of the input are deleted. Then step I is, quite:
Step I: sherr qua pi b
Step II: the last letter of the words of input are prefixed to it:
y nt r
Step III: all the vowels of the words of the input are deleted:
shrr qt pnt br
Let us see so far
Now let us now have a look at yet another machine and see its output.
Suppose our input is as follows:
Input: 4 7 9 18 13
And suppose our various outputs are:
1st step: 4 7 5 4
2nd step: 7 9 5 9
3rd step: 169 225 36 361 196
4th step: 13 15 6 19 14
Clearly, the various steps are obtained as per the following rules.
(i) the 1st step is obtained by adding the digits of the numbers of the input.
(ii) the 2nd step is obtained by multiplying the input by 3.
(iii) the 3rd step is obtained by adding 1 to the input numbers and then squaring it.
(iv) the fourth step is obtained by doubling the input.
For example consider the last number of the input. It is 13. In the 1st step, we add its two digits: We get, 1 + 3 = 4. In the second step we multiply it by 3: 13 + 39. In the next step we add 1 and then square: (13 + 1)² = (14)² = 196. In the last step we multiply it by 2: 13 × 2 = 26.
The four basic types of operations
Having discussed the foregoing four examples, we are now in a position to focus on four basic types of questions that are usually asked. They are:
SHIFTING: In this type of questions, we usually shift the given words (or numbers) of the given input as per a fixed pattern.
DELETING: In this type we do it already. In Ex.1, we had the first two words shifting their places in operation 1 and then the last two words shifting their places in operation Two. This was an example of shifting.
ARRANGING: In this type of questions, the words or the numbers are arranged as per a fixed order. This order can be an alphabetical order in case of words; it can be an increasing or decreasing order in case of numbers. Note that whereas shifting goes on endlessly; arranging ends as soon as the order intended is achieved.
As an example of arranging is Ex 2. In this we saw that the given input was arranged alphabetically in subsequent steps.
